title: Ammosperma cinereum
uses: Ammosperma cinereum, also known as the gray buckwheat, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Traditionally, its roots were used to treat fever and rheumatism. The plant's roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, Ammosperma cinereum has been used to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these traditional uses.
taxonomy: Ammosperma cinereum is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification reflects its shared characteristics with other plants in this family, such as composite flowers and similar leaf structures. Ammosperma cinereum is also a member of the tribe Heliantheae, a group of flowering plants known for their daisy-like flowers and similar growth habits. The classification of Ammosperma cinereum helps to understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants and its place within the plant kingdom.

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating various health issues such as inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Ammosperma cinereum include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ammosperma cinereum is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, extracts, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies, such as poultices and salves, to alleviate various health conditions.
